1.main_content:
Hello World! This is my first real blog post so it will probably be a bit awkward. Today was nice, it was really sunny and warm, in spite of strong wind.

I wanted to spend some time outside so I decided to clean up some trash in the lawn and cleaned the bird feeders so I can set them up now that winter is over. I'm looking forward to getting back into birding, I've been too stressed the last few months to make time for it.

GF and I got groceries which was nice, especially the yummy cream buns they sell. We watched the end of the Pesci fight in Jojo part V, really a top tier fight. The train is a really cool setting for a stand fight, Araki must have thought so too given it comes back for part 7. 

We're going swimming tonight which will be fun, even though I'm quite sore from a new workout routine. Overall it's been a really nice day.

2.main_content:
Today was the last day of my break before my final semester of college, so I was a bit bummed out when I woke up, so I took a trip over to the gym to feel better. The weather has finally gotten nice enough for me to walk to the gym regularly and it has been a huge boost to my mood, vitamin D is a hell of a drug. I tried some new core exercises to challenge myself today, which went great, until an hour later when it hurt to cough.

I've been playing Gambonanza, which is a shameless ripoff of Balatro, but with Chess, but it does a really good job of ripping off the best parts of Balatro (relaxing atmosphere, effective graphics, interesting synergies). I'm having fun with it, but its also making me feel really stupid at chess :p

Regan and I made a delicious fried rice for dinner, we added chicken breast to our regular recipe and it did a lot to improve both the flavor and portion size of the meal, which was very nice. Regan added cream to the scrambled eggs, which made them extra fluffy and boosted the flavor of the rice by a lot.

3.main_content:
First day of the semester. Pulled over for bullshit. Car troubles. Water-boiler exploded. Infinite hell forever.




:)

4.main_content:
Today has been a pretty good day. We finally got out to Costco after coasting without groceries for far too long, and managed to make it out without spending all our money which was nice. The summer weather has me wanting to grill, so we picked up some sausages and pork skewers.

We made a delicious German Chocolate cake last night(which is not German, nor chocolatey) using a recipe from sugar geeks (https://sugargeekshow.com/german-chocolate-cake/#recipe). In addition to a typical German Chocolate cake, this recipe adds a chocolatey meringue, which is not needed, but well worth the effort. It was illegally good.

We started a new TV marathon recently, and I think its one of our strongest so far. In addition to hitting some really good arcs in existing series (i.e., Evangelion 20, Cowboy Bebop 20, Futurama season 4, Steven Universe season 3), we had some really good new series like Dragon ball Daima, Clerks Animated and Serial Experiments Lain. I've been particularly interested in Lain, I've always had an interest in it, but never knew anything about it other than major motifs of y2k technology. The dark themes and abstract story telling make for an extremely compelling that leaves me glued to the screen every time it comes around.

We're going swimming again tonight which is always exciting; its been really nice having a consistent physical activity for Regan and I to do together. I've been doing a lot of miscellaneous physical activity lately which has been a nice change of pace from my regular gym routines. Overall today has been good.

5.main_content:
It was Regan's birthday recently, so we went to go see The Mandalorian and Grogu with her mom. I haven't watched the TV series, so my expectations going into it was for more sequel-trilogy-area slop. I was pleasantly surprised, however, as this movie is divorced from the baggage of post-Disney corporate-extended-universe Star-Wars; not even one time did a character utter the words "Skywalker", "Palpatine" or even "light-saber".

Starting with the good, the use of puppetry for Grogu and some of the other smaller creatures was much more pleasing to watch compared to uncanny CGI ghouls of other recent Star-Wars media. The dynamic of Mando as the gruff mercenary, whose heart is melted by the purity of their chipper young companion of Grogu is a classic trope, but a well executed for this movie, as it creates compelling character motivations. The action sequences were well choreographed, making for an exciting watch, especially the big pit fight scene. Steve Blum made an unexpected, but very enjoyable appearance voicing a witty co-pilot for Mando, they have good chemistry and he's got a great voice for the role.

Next, the weird and the bad. First the weird: Jabba the Hut's son is voiced by a Seth Rogen Impersonator. I have no source to confirm this, but he sounds like 10% different than Seth Rogen, and has the same dude-bro cadence, its amazing he never did the laugh. As far as the bad, the pacing felt strange in the middle, as if the movie were two TV scripts stitched together with a 15 minute interlude. It could have been a bit smoother transitioning between the end of the first rescue mission and the start of the arc with the assassin. My biggest personal gripe was the scene where Mando's mask is removed, because in spite of Pedro Pascal's good looks, Mando is the kind of masked character who is extremely hot, but instantly loses all sex-appeal once they remove their mask/helmet (i.e., Master Chief, Zorro, any male Knight). Another issue me and Regan both had was with the IMAX theater, I don't know if I'm getting old, but it was far too loud at times, especially during the pre-movie ads (shocking). Also there were some scenes with extreme flashing lights which were a problem.

Overall it was a very enjoyable experience (especially since it was free), and a refreshing reminder that under the right circumstances, Star Wars can be good, actually. Happy Birthday Regan :3
